extra.reason: Replaced git_revwalk with git_odb_foreach in hanami_push_to_blossom to enumerate all object types (commits, trees, blobs, tags). Extracted callback to file-scope static function for C11 compliance.
Fixed by deferring cap update until after successful realloc. Now cap only changes when the allocation succeeds.
Removed contradictory CURLOPT_UPLOAD and CURLOPT_INFILESIZE_LARGE. Now uses only CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST=PUT + CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS.

Fixed 4 memory leaks: (1) ODB backend freed when git_repository_odb fails. (2) RefDB backend freed when git_refdb attach fails. (3) nostr_ctx freed when refdb creation fails. (4) Error* freed in ensure_relays on relay connection failure. (5) event->id freed before overwrite in sign_event.

Fixed: (1) Detached HEAD now published as raw OID hex. (2) malloc failures in publish_state now skip the ref (continue) instead of sending NULL target. Remaining: cache refresh and network error silencing tracked separately.

Implemented timeline binder policy gating in apps/gnostr/src/ui/gnostr-timeline-view-app-factory.c: pre-hides newly bound rows, evaluates GnostrCardVisibilityPolicy for final visibility, reconnects notify::profile based on actual profile absence, and recomputes visibility after profile arrival while preserving row lifecycle disconnects. Fixed all four timeline card issues: blank cards on startup (hide row in setup), scroll jitter (immediate upper-growth compensation), muted avatars (removed opaque inline CSS background), and non-uniform margins (uniform 8px margin)
Added nostr_gtk_note_card_row_get_avatar_widgets() accessor and gnostr_avatar_download_async() calls in both the Tier 2 map handler and on_item_profile_changed callback in note-card-factory.c
Implemented GnostrTimelineSource/GnostrTimelineBatch and legacy GnNostrEventModel adapter; gnostr and event-model-windowing targets built. Focused runtime still hits existing storage teardown crash.

Implemented GnostrTimelineFeedController core with source ingestion, hidden working set, pending-head semantics, snapshot publication, anchor math, geometry cache, signals, and focused feed-controller tests passing.

Implemented snapshot-row binding in gnostr-timeline-view-app-factory with reserved geometry/token before content, no mutable snapshot-row wiring, measured-geometry cleanup, and controller geometry forwarding when attached; gnostr target builds.

Implemented rich snapshot row rendering from immutable VM data and fixed review blockers: snapshot rows bind precomputed markup/context/count/action state, reserved media/link areas are inert placeholders, no snapshot request-embed/DB/OG/media fetch from bind, focused timeline tests pass.

Standalone meson build now links signetd/signetctl + all tests: mirrored CMake's in-tree archive link line (correct names/order) + curl/nsync/secp256k1/websockets/flatccrt. meson compile builds the daemon; 9/9 meson tests pass.

Implemented: mgmt REVOKE_AGENT now resolves the agent pubkey and calls signet_revoke_agent (deny-list add + lease burn + key wipe + audit) via the shared live deny list wired through signet_mgmt_handler_set_deny_list in signetd_main. Falls back to cache-only key wipe when no persistent store. Builds clean; deny-precedence e2e test tracked by nostrc-4dp.

Implemented: POST /bootstrap now calls signet_store_consume_bootstrap_token after minting the URI, atomically marking the token used (UPDATE ... WHERE used_at IS NULL). Concurrent/replayed redemptions are rejected with 403 token already used. Builds clean; single-use regression test tracked by nostrc-4dp.

Implemented: signet_mgmt_publish_ack now fails closed — if NIP-44 encryption of the ACK fails or no recipient resolves, it logs a warning and refuses to publish, never falling back to plaintext. Builds clean and all signet tests pass.

INVALID as filed / already implemented. SIGHUP-driven policy reload IS wired: signetd calls signet_policy_store_file_new (signetd_main.c:610), which self-installs a SIGHUP handler (policy_store.c:1018) that sets g_signet_policy_sighup_requested and requests audit-log reopen (policy_store.c:85). On the request path signet_policy_engine_eval -> signet_policy_store_get -> signet_policy_store_maybe_reload_locked (policy_store.c:651,593) consumes the flag and reloads the policy file. The signetd header comment is accurate: reload is intentionally handled in the policy module, not signetd_main. Verified by tracing the eval path.
Implemented and built; see commit. p0s: all sub-items addressed (secure wipes, rotate store lookup, policy-default clarification, audit-chain doc scope).

Implemented and built; see commit. p0s: all sub-items addressed (secure wipes, rotate store lookup, policy-default clarification, audit-chain doc scope).

Implemented runtime SQLCipher detection + keyed-read validation + optional hard enforcement (SIGNET_REQUIRE_ENCRYPTED_DB) + meson -Dsignet_use_sqlcipher build option. Default plain-sqlite build now warns loudly instead of silently claiming encryption. Remaining: connect_secret is still plaintext when SQLCipher is off (mitigated by the warning/enforcement; envelope-encrypting connect_secret tracked separately if desired). At-rest test tracked by nostrc-23g.

Fixed the P0 authz leak: GetToken now enforces per-agent ownership (returns NotFound + audits deny on mismatch) across D-Bus Unix and TCP; store-less GetSession refuses instead of minting an untracked token. NOTE: the store-backed GetSession still issues a Signet-minted, lease-tracked bearer token rather than performing an external service credential-exchange (the session_broker path, hardened in nostrc-e5y, does real exchange but takes a credential_id, not a service_url). Wiring D-Bus GetSession through a real service exchange is a design enhancement, not a leak; deferred.

Added signet/tests/test_signing_crypto.c (wired into meson): proves signing correctness (valid Schnorr sig + pubkey/id match), NIP-44 round-trip with tamper + wrong-key rejection, and envelope encryption at rest (plaintext absent from disk bytes). SQLCipher-specific at-rest bytes (wrong-key rejection on an encrypted DB) require a sqlcipher-linked build; the generic envelope-encryption proof runs in the default build.

Implemented [uid_map] config section + signetd_uid_resolver wired into D-Bus Unix and SSH agent production configs. Peer UID (SO_PEERCRED/getpeereid) now maps to an agent_id; unmapped UIDs are rejected. Documented in signet.conf.example.

Both halves done. Rate-limit test (earlier) asserts real enforcement/isolation. Auth test now constructs a real signed kind-28100 challenge event and asserts SIGNET_AUTH_OK plus full negative coverage (replay, bad sig, wrong kind/purpose, unknown/expired challenge, deny precedence, pubkey mismatch). While writing it, found+fixed a latent bug where auth deserialize used an uninstalled JSON backend (tracked as nostrc-3x5).
Fixed: nostr_auth.c now uses nostr_event_deserialize_compact (backend-independent). Proven by test_nostr_auth positive case which passes without any JSON backend installed.

Added test_store_lifecycle.c (wired into meson): proves audit hash-chain integrity + tamper detection, bootstrap token single-use (guards ufh), and revoke->deny-list precedence + key wipe (guards sd3). Replay-through-full-NIP46-server path deferred (requires relay+encryption harness); the isolated replay INVALID behavior is covered by the replay_cache test and the nip46 mapping.

Implemented per-agent envelope keys in store_secrets (put/get/rotate): each credential payload is encrypted under a keyed-BLAKE2b subkey derived from the store DEK + the owning agent_pubkey, so cross-agent decryption is impossible by construction. Deterministic from the row's agent_pubkey; keys wiped after use. README 'per-agent libsodium secretbox' claim is now accurate. NOTE: changes the at-rest format vs the old store-wide-DEK scheme (fresh DBs; no production migration path added).

Fixed the concrete UV bug: CTAP-HID now honors allow_headless_uv (uv=true passes through to the service instead of being rejected outright). Verified with passkeys-enabled build (14/14 tests). The other items are by-design, not defects: signCount=0 is intentional for synced/portable passkey credentials (documented in store_passkeys.h; incrementing would break the sync model); ES256-only matches the advertised COSE alg in getInfo; origin/RP validation is the client/browser's WebAuthn responsibility — the authenticator correctly derives rpIdHash from rp_id. Documented in issue.

Addressed the core gap: added .github/workflows/signet-ci.yml — signet previously had NO CI (main CI uses SIGNET_ENABLE=OFF). The workflow builds signet via the monorepo CMake and runs the full ctest suite, with a matrix entry (SIGNET_ENABLE_PASSKEYS=ON) so passkey/FIDO tests run in CI. Also wired the new crypto + store-lifecycle tests into CMakeLists so meson and CMake stay in sync (verified 16/16 ctest pass locally, incl. fido_service/fido_ctaphid/store_passkeys). DEFERRED (documented): test_fido_service/test_fido_interop_json still use a lightweight SignetStore shim for unit isolation (they do exercise the real fido/CBOR/store_passkeys code); the live_dbus_interop test remains a manual/disabled harness (needs a running daemon + system bus).

All 23 child issues resolved on branch signet/security-hardening-2026-07-03 (+ 1 discovered latent bug nostrc-3x5). P0: SQLCipher enforcement (1ww), bootstrap single-use (ufh), revoke deny-list (sd3), D-Bus credential ownership (8q2). P1: relay publish false-success (593), plaintext ACK fail-closed (aoe), session_broker fail-closed (e5y), SO_PEERCRED uid_resolver (t04), SSH key-blob binding (zi6), SIGHUP reload verified (lt1), nip44 handlers (hx9), relay-recv verify (o02), transactional rotation (5tc), real rate/auth tests (dhd), crypto/at-rest tests (23g), + auth JSON-backend bug (3x5). P2: set_policy code (d75), replay fail-closed (fao), DB-key/KDF docs (9np), per-agent DEK (op9), secure-wipe grab-bag (p0s), FIDO UV (aff), e2e tests (4dp), signet CI (a6g). Test suite grew from 5 to 10 meson tests (16 CMake ctests incl passkeys); all pass. Signet now builds clean and is materially more real.
Added GTK-Doc coverage for Signet public API headers and docs scaffold; validation/build checks passed.

Fixed: nostr_key_is_valid_public now decodes the 32-byte x-only key and validates via secp256k1_xonly_pubkey_parse instead of treating it as a 33-byte compressed key. Regression test libnostr/tests/test_core_audit.c (test_pubkey_validation) asserts a generated pubkey validates true; builds+passes under CTest (libnostr_core_audit).

Fixed: tb_allow now rejects non-finite and negative cost (returns false without mutating bucket state) before any token math. Regression test test_rate_limiter_cost asserts negative/NaN/Inf denied with no inflation and normal burst throttling. Note: shared-bucket thread-safety is documented as single-owner; a lock/atomic option can be a follow-up if buckets are ever shared.

Fixed: nostr_bip39_seed now sizes the PBKDF2 salt dynamically (8 + strlen(passphrase)), checks nfkd_dup() for NULL, and OPENSSL_cleanse()s the salt. Verified against the standard BIP-39 Trezor test vector AND a regression asserting two 300-byte passphrases differing past byte 256 derive different seeds (previously identical due to char[9+256] truncation). test_bip39_seed passes.

Fixed: default_deserialize_event in json_backend_libjson.c now returns 0 on success / -1 on failure, matching the facade (json.c) and every sibling function in the file. Previously inverted (1=success/0=failure) so fallback-path valid JSON was treated as failure and malformed JSON as success. Verified by code inspection + build; a fallback-path integration test is tracked under nostrc-9lo.

Fixed: ndb_backend ln_ndb_open now refuses ingest_skip_validation unless NOSTR_ALLOW_UNSAFE_INGEST=1 is set in the environment, and logs a loud warning when active (and when refused). A stray production opts_json key can no longer disable id/signature validation. Test harnesses (tests/testkit/gnostr-testkit.c, tests/test_ndb_ingestion_simple.c) updated to set the env var explicitly. Builds clean.
Updated Signet docs to describe ContextVM 25910 management intents and mark 28000-series as legacy compatibility.
Escaped event id/pubkey/sig and relay OK/CLOSED/EOSE string fields; added JSON-validity regression coverage.
Fixed compact event/envelope parsers to require required fields and reject malformed/trailing/truncated input; added parsing audit regressions.
Reused shared JSON string parsing for filters, escaped control chars on output, bounded int arrays, and added round-trip/overflow regressions.

Fixed networking reliability issues: pthread_create failure now tears down the new LWS context/connection and returns NULL; request enqueue and connect result wait are bounded, with cancellation/ref ownership to avoid queued-request UAF; write_message now NULL-guards err on the late close path. Covered by libnostr_networking_audit for err==NULL paths; thread-start failure and live LWS stall paths verified by inspection because deterministic resource-exhaustion/service-stall unit tests are not practical here. Built nostr + test_networking_audit and ran direct test + ctest.

Completed remaining networking portions: nostr_relay_connect now discards/frees freshly-created NostrConnection on post-connect error paths and unwinds both pre-ref'd contexts on worker-arg allocation failure; relay_optimized frees event worker_ctx, checks create_batch calloc, frees WebSocketMessage/batches on failed channel sends, and avoids verify-send double-free. Realloc-loses-pointer parts were already fixed in prior commit. Added ASan-friendly relay connect error-path regression in libnostr_networking_audit; relay_optimized optional source syntax-checked separately because it is not part of the nostr target. Built nostr + test_networking_audit and ran direct test + ctest.
Fixed nostrdb TLS transaction cache and live-handle tracking to be scoped per struct ndb handle; added two-store regression test.

bip32.c: zeroize all sensitive stack material (I/IL/IR, private key k, chain code c, HMAC input data) on every return path via ok/goto-cleanup + OPENSSL_cleanse; clear secret BIGNUMs (kpar/IL/child) with BN_clear_free in add_mod_n and the IL range check. bip39.c: wordlist lazy-init now race-free via pthread_once (replaced g_words_init flag). Added libnostr/tests/test_crypto_audit.c: BIP-32 vector 1 (m and m/0'/1/2'/2/1000000000) proves derivation unchanged, plus 16-thread concurrent validate/generate race test. Builds clean; libnostr_crypto_audit passes.

metrics_collector.c: stop() now sets a shutdown flag, wakes the worker via condvar, and pthread_join()s it BEFORE freeing export_path (UAF fixed); start() checks pthread_create and does not report running on failure (frees export_path). metrics.c: dump/prometheus now flush ALL threads' counter caches (tls_counters_flush_all) via a global registry, not just the caller's — per-thread cache moved from __thread to a heap block owned by a pthread_key (macOS dyld frees __thread storage before key destructors, which ASan confirmed as a heap-UAF); cross-thread drain uses release/acquire on 'used' + atomic_exchange on pending. Regression test libnostr/tests/test_misc_audit.c (compiled with NOSTR_ENABLE_METRICS=1): 200x start/stop cycle + 8-thread live-cache aggregation, ASan-clean under 15x stress.

security_limits_runtime.c: read_ll_env now uses strtoll with endptr + errno(ERANGE) validation, rejects trailing junk ('123abc') and overflow (21-digit), requires positive, clamps sanely, falls back deterministically; all limit caches resolved once via pthread_once (first-call race fixed). storage_registry.c: g_head list now guarded by a mutex in register/create, factory invoked outside the lock. Regression tests in test_misc_audit.c: malformed/overflow/valid env parsing via public getters, concurrent limit reads, and an 8-thread x 2000-iter register/create storm — all pass ASan-clean.

libnostr now has 6 real compiled CTest suites (core/parsing/networking/store_txn/crypto/misc) wired into CI: nostrc-ci.yml runs them via an explicit 'libnostr audit tests' ctest step AND the full Test step, building with GO_WARNINGS_AS_ERRORS=ON + LIBNOSTR_WITH_NOSTRDB=ON. Verified all six build -Werror-clean and pass locally. store_txn test guarded behind LIBNOSTR_WITH_NOSTRDB; GI job opts out via LIBNOSTR_BUILD_TESTS=OFF. Orphan dead test removed earlier. Root problem (no compiled tests / fake tests masking defects) fully resolved.

All 15 child issues resolved. Delivered: fixed broken pubkey validation, inverted JSON backend contract, rate-limiter throttle bypass, BIP-39 passphrase truncation, reachable signature-skip; hardened compact deserializers, event/relay serialization escaping, filter escape handling; scoped nostrdb TLS transactions per store; zeroized bip32 secrets + race-free bip39 init; fixed metrics-collector UAF + cross-thread aggregation; hardened env parsing + registry/limit-cache locking; fixed realloc/leak/hang bugs in relay/connection paths. Added 6 real CTest suites wired into CI (-Werror clean). All work on branch libnostr-production-hardening.

Fixed in windows-build.yml: nsync now builds with POSIX/pthread backend on MSYS2 mingw64. Verification pending CI re-run (no local Windows runner).
Completed: Signet Go SDK uses cascadia.CAS_AUDIT on cascadia-go v0.2.1; go mod tidy, go build ./..., and go test ./... passed. C daemon residual remains tracked in nostrc-e5d.
Fixed: Signet C audit publishers now use generated CAS_AUDIT constant; cmake --build build --target signetd signetctl passes.
Fixed sqlite constructor to fail and return NULL when encryption_key is requested without SQLITE_HAS_CODEC; added storage contract regression for encrypted opens without codec.
Checked mandatory storage writes in group/message/welcome/key-package public API paths, propagating storage errors and adding best-effort cleanup; added failing-storage API regressions.
Added public API storage/vtable-slot guards for pending welcomes/groups/messages, media encrypt/decrypt, and related storage entry points; partial backends now return MARMOT_ERR_STORAGE.
Memory snapshot create/rollback/release/prune now return MARMOT_ERR_UNSUPPORTED with tests documenting unsupported semantics.
Implemented real MDK JSON loaders/assertions; interop now fails on genuine secret-tree production mismatch instead of green-theater pass
Corrected misleading vector provenance comments; JSON-backed coverage is in test_interop

Fixed RFC 9420 secret-tree sender ratchet derivation: per-generation key/nonce/secret now use DeriveTreeSecret with uint32 big-endian generation context; sender-data sampling now uses KDF.Nh (32 bytes for suite 1) instead of AEAD key length. MDK secret-tree vectors pass.
Fixed marmot-gobject welcome accept flow, full field marshalling, signal emission, enum/storage semantics, relay access, and media metadata; added behavioral tests.

Wave 5 commit 47342562: bounded skipped-key cache for OOO decrypt + replay rejection; forward-secret erasure of node/leaf/ratchet secrets; single-parse sender-data decrypt with pre-consumption validation. Full marmot suite 19/19 PASS; OOO/beyond-window/replay/erasure tests added.

Commit 10dc6fff: mls_message_deserialize now propagates PUBLIC/PRIVATE parse errors (opaque preservation kept only for WELCOME/GROUP_INFO/KEY_PACKAGE); this exposed+fixed 2 real parser defects (ProposalOrRef refs, UpdatePathNode.encryption_key VLBytes) so interop passes via REAL parsing; malformed-PublicMessage rejection test added. sqlite/nostrdb snapshot ops now fail-closed (MARMOT_ERR_UNSUPPORTED). 19/19 marmot.

Commit 10dc6fff: mls_message_deserialize now propagates PUBLIC/PRIVATE parse errors (opaque preservation kept only for WELCOME/GROUP_INFO/KEY_PACKAGE); this exposed+fixed 2 real parser defects (ProposalOrRef refs, UpdatePathNode.encryption_key VLBytes) so interop passes via REAL parsing; malformed-PublicMessage rejection test added. sqlite/nostrdb snapshot ops now fail-closed (MARMOT_ERR_UNSUPPORTED). 19/19 marmot.

Root cause: no-path commit confirmed-transcript-hash used a raw byte-scan that included the 2-byte MLSMessage version prefix, diverging from RFC 9420 §8.1 ConfirmedTranscriptHashInput; corrupted GroupContext→epoch authenticator+confirmation tag for all no-path (Add-only) commits (desync bug). Fix (commit 19236821): both branches reconstruct input via public_message_confirmed_transcript_input; byte-scan helper deleted; strict confirmation check intact. Verified against MDK passive-client vector (0d885d8f...). 19/19 marmot.

All 5 formerly-deferred MDK vector classes now assert real bytes: psk_secret(11 chains, new mls_psk_secret_compute), tree-operations(5 replays), tree-validation(14 trees), treekem(62 UpdatePaths/11 cases), passive-client(22 welcome-joins + 22 per-epoch commits; 13 cases fully asserted). Commits c8582ab8/51667bfe/19236821. Exposed+fixed real defects: leaf-clone lifetime, RatchetTree TLS trim/extend, parent-hash chain validation, UpdatePath-HPKE-GroupContext(§7.6), filtered-direct-path child, no-path confirmed-transcript-hash(§8.1). 9 honest passive-client sub-case XFAILs (PSK/GCE-in-commit + truncated-path edge) tracked in nostrc-2ts. 19/19 marmot.

All 9 passive-client residuals resolved (commits b77c134d + baa13cbd). Implemented PSK proposal application (collect in wire order, resolve external/resumption PSKs, mls_psk_secret_compute → injected into joiner_secret via group_derive_epoch) + resumption-PSK cache + GroupContextExtensions application; and truncated filtered-direct-path handling (canonical trimmed tree-hash view preserving live indices, unified UpdatePath apply, own_path_keys cache). passive-client now 22/22 fully asserted, 0 XFAIL, 226 per-epoch commits; deferred vector classes: 0; 10373 MDK asserted checks. 19/19 marmot.

All children resolved: waves 1-5 crypto + Track B protocol/storage + review WEAK items + full MDK cross-validation (all 13 vector classes assert real bytes, 10373 checks, 0 deferred). libmarmot/marmot-gobject production-ready; 19/19 marmot suite; independent adversarial review passed. Remaining honest note: unrelated pre-existing gnostr-app linker error outside libmarmot.

Implemented: legacy plaintext SQLite -> SQLCipher migration. store.c detects the plaintext magic header, runtime-probes SQLCipher availability, and migrates via ATTACH KEY + sqlcipher_export with EXCLUSIVE lock, WAL checkpoint, verify+fsync, crash-safe hard-link/byte-copy backup, sidecar cleanup before atomic rename, and key wiping. signet_store_open auto-migrates by default (SIGNET_MIGRATE_PLAINTEXT_DB=false to refuse); signetctl migrate-db for explicit runs. CMake -DSIGNET_USE_SQLCIPHER option + CI sqlcipher matrix added. test_db_migration passes in both plain (SKIP real migration) and sqlcipher (real migration, data preserved, backup kept, idempotent) builds; verified e2e on a 1000-row WAL DB. Docs updated.

Fixed: added nips/nip17/src/nip17.c to meson signet_core_sources; signetd/signetctl now link (no more undefined _nostr_nip17_wrap_dm/decrypt_dm). Verified 11/11 meson tests + binaries link in plain and sqlcipher builds.

Fixed in libnostr/src/connection.c: the lws service loop now requests a writable callback for all client connections each iteration (lws_callback_on_writable_all_protocol), so queued frames are always flushed even when the writable_pending/EVENT_WAIT_CANCELLED per-wsi drain misses after a reconnect. signetctl also subscribes after connect to reduce churn. signetctl provision now works fully standalone end-to-end (intent reaches relay, daemon provisions + replies, signetctl prints ContextVM result). No regressions across signet + libnostr/relay/subscription/concurrency tests.

Implemented and verified end-to-end standalone against a default daemon (mgmt_legacy_28000=false). signetctl builds ContextVM JSON-RPC intents (kind-25910 payload: method agent/*, params agent_id/policy/deliver+bootstrap_pubkey+delivery_ttl), gift-wraps them via NIP-59 (kind 1059) to the bunker, and consumes the gift-wrapped ContextVM reply (unwrap + bunker-sender verify + JSON-RPC id correlation + result/error). Added [nostr] bunker_pubkey config + SIGNET_BUNKER_PUBKEY env so signetctl addresses the bunker with only the provisioner key. Blocking publish bug nostrc-7k6 fixed. Real e2e: signetctl provision returns a live bunker:// URI.

Implemented signetd_subscribe_mgmt_kinds helper used at all three subscription sites (initial + 2 resubscribe paths). Default (legacy off) subscribes only to [24133,25910,1059]; legacy_28000=true adds 28000-28050. Verified live via relay REQ capture. Also fixed a latent bug: the resubscribe arrays were missing 25910/1059 (management broke after reconnect) and weren't #p-scoped — now consistent. signet 11/11 tests pass.

Fixed: added SignetStoreConfig.read_only (SQLITE_OPEN_READONLY + skip schema/migration/WAL + PRAGMA query_only), used by signetctl's pure-read introspection commands; added busy_timeout(5s) and surfaced the real sqlite error (g_warning + sqlite3_errmsg in keyed-read failure). Verified list-agents now works against a live daemon holding the same DB in both plain-SQLite and SQLCipher builds; 11/11 tests pass. rotate-credential still opens read-write.
Closed

Implemented agent/reissue-connect end-to-end: store+key_store helpers, ContextVM 25910 method, signetctl reissue-connect command (--out atomic 0600 write / --show-secret), Go SDK method, docs, tests all passing. Follow-ups filed: nostrc-oe9 (standalone helper if signetctl insufficient), nostrc-6e9 (mgmt-plane replay protection).

Wired replay protection into the mgmt handler: signet_mgmt_handler_set_replay_cache, check-and-mark by delivered event id after parse/before execute (covers ContextVM + legacy paths via handle_event funnel); duplicates silently dropped, missing event id fails closed with replay_invalid ack; signetd uses a dedicated mgmt replay cache (not shared with NIP-46) so signing load cannot evict entries. test_mgmt_replay.c covers duplicate rejection, replayed reissue-connect minting exactly once, and fail-closed missing id. Oracle-reviewed; ack-race and cache-eviction findings addressed.
